preamble.summary: Esau's sons incite him to wage war against Jacob. Despite the oath to Isaac, Esau gathers a vast army. Jacob sends messengers of peace, but Esau refuses. Judah fights valiantly. Jacob shoots Esau with an arrow.
preamble.remarkable: This entire war narrative has NO parallel in Genesis. It likely reflects Hasmonean-era conflicts between Judea and Idumea.
preamble.friction: The wars directly contradict the peaceful separation in Genesis 33 and 36.
preamble.connections: Genesis 33 (peaceful reunion — contradicted); 1 Maccabees 5 (possible historical backdrop); Obadiah 1 (oracle against Edom).

verses.text_reference: And the sons of Esau heard that Isaac had given the portion of the elder to Jacob.
verses.rendering: On the day Isaac died, the sons of Esau heard that Isaac had given the elder son's portion to Jacob. They were furious.

verses.text_reference: And they strove with their father, saying: 'Why has thy father given Jacob the inheritance?'
verses.rendering: They argued with Esau: Why did your father give Jacob the inheritance? You are the firstborn!

verses.text_reference: And he said: 'Because I sold my birthright to Jacob.'
verses.rendering: Esau said: Because I sold my birthright to Jacob for a bowl of lentils. And he came with cunning and my father blessed him.

verses.text_reference: 'And our father made us swear not to devise evil against each other.'
verses.rendering: Now our father made both of us swear that neither would plot evil against the other.

verses.text_reference: And they said: 'We will not hearken. Our strength is greater than his.'
verses.rendering: They said: We will not listen to you about making peace. Our strength is greater than his. We will march against him and kill him.

verses.text_reference: 'Let us send to Aram and Philistia and Moab and Ammon for picked men of war.'
verses.rendering: Let us send word to Aram, Philistia, Moab, and Ammon. Let us recruit fighting men and march against him.

verses.text_reference: And their father said: 'Do not go, lest ye fall before him.'
verses.rendering: Their father said: Do not go. Do not wage war on him, or you will fall before him.

verses.text_reference: And they said: 'This is thy manner — thou puttest thy neck under his yoke.'
verses.rendering: They said: This is how you have always been — putting your neck under his yoke. We will not listen.

verses.text_reference: And they sent to Aram and hired one thousand fighting men.
verses.rendering: They sent to Aram and Aduram and hired one thousand fighting men — chosen warriors.

verses.text_reference: And from Moab and Ammon and Philistia and Edom came chosen men of war.
verses.rendering: From Moab and the Ammonites came one thousand hired warriors. From Philistia and Edom, another thousand each. And from the Kittim came mighty men.
translator_notes: The Kittim — a code name for Western powers. In the Dead Sea Scrolls, the Kittim are Rome.

verses.text_reference: And they said to Esau: 'Go forth with them, or we will slay thee.'
verses.rendering: They said to their father: Go out with them and lead them — or we will kill you.

verses.text_reference: And he was filled with wrath at being forced to go against his brother.
verses.rendering: Esau was filled with anger, seeing that his sons were forcing him to march against his brother Jacob.

verses.text_reference: And he remembered the evil in his heart and forgot his oath.
verses.rendering: He remembered the resentment hidden in his heart and forgot the oath he had sworn to his father and mother.

verses.text_reference: And Jacob knew not that they were coming. He was mourning for Leah.
verses.rendering: Jacob did not know they were coming in war. He was mourning for his wife Leah, until they came close with four thousand warriors.

verses.text_reference: And the men of Hebron sent to him: 'Thy brother cometh with four thousand men.'
verses.rendering: The people of Hebron sent word: Your brother has come against you with four thousand armed men.

verses.text_reference: And Jacob sent to Esau: 'Come and let us make peace.'
verses.rendering: Jacob sent word to Esau: Come to me and let us make peace. If they need land, I will give them territory.

verses.text_reference: And Esau said: 'I have no need of thy goods. Come to me in war.'
verses.rendering: Esau replied: I have no need of your goods. Come and face me in battle.

verses.text_reference: And Jacob sent again: 'Do me not this evil.'
verses.rendering: Jacob sent a second time: I beg you, brother, do not do this evil. The Lord will not grant you the right to fight me.

verses.text_reference: And Esau said: 'I will go against him in battle.'
verses.rendering: Esau said to himself: I will not listen. I will march against him.

verses.text_reference: And Esau advanced against Jacob.
verses.rendering: Esau advanced against Jacob, and Jacob went out to meet him with a large force.

verses.text_reference: And Judah went first and took his bow.
verses.rendering: Judah took the lead. He took up his bow and went out into the field. He and his men struck down the forces of Esau.
translator_notes: Judah as premier warrior anticipates the Maccabean ideology.

verses.text_reference: And Judah pursued Esau and his arrows pierced him.
verses.rendering: Judah pursued Esau, and his arrows flew at him, piercing him.

verses.text_reference: And Jacob drew a bow and shot Esau and slew him.
verses.rendering: Jacob drew his bow and shot his brother Esau, striking him in the right side of the chest. He killed him.
translator_notes: Jacob killing Esau is an extraordinary departure from Genesis, where Esau dies peacefully.

verses.text_reference: And he pursued Esau's sons, and they fled before him.
verses.rendering: He pursued Esau's sons and struck them down, and they fled before him.

verses.text_reference: And Jacob prevailed, and the sons of Esau accepted subjection.
verses.rendering: Jacob prevailed over the sons of his brother Esau, and they submitted to him.
